channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The DNC chair candidates discuss Joe Biden, Kamala Harris and winning Latino voters
item.description: Four Democrats are running for Democratic National Committee chair, looking to take on the task of reinvigorating a party demoralized by a second loss to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bureau of Prisons agrees to court monitor, public acknowledgement of staff-on-inmate sexual abuse
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A legal settlement in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse will force the federal Bureau of Prisons to open its doors to a court-appointed monitor and publicly acknowledge pervasive misconduct at its now-shuttered women’s prison in California.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MICHAEL BALSAMO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother of Austin Tice, journalist missing in Syria, says new information proves her son is alive
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The mother of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for more than a decade, said Friday that she was confident her son was alive, citing information she said had come from a “significant source” that she did not identify but said had been vetted by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on women in combat.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Military leaders are rattled by a list of 'woke' officers that a group urges Hegseth to fire
item.description: The list compiled by the American Accountability Foundation includes 20 general officers or senior admirals and a disproportionate number of female officers.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Detransitioners, parents demand end to ‘butchery’ of children through sex-change surgeries
item.description: Former transgenders, parents and activists braved cold temperatures on Wednesday morning to rally outside the Supreme Court to demand an end to the “butchery" of child sex change surgeries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daniel Penny to be tapped for Congressional Gold Medal by House GOP lawmaker
item.description: Rep. Eli Crane is planning to introduce a resolution to honor a U.S. Marine veteran on trial for killing a homeless man who was described as acting erratic on the subway.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson woos his sharpest critics in preparation for Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is wooing his sharpest critics in the House GOP ahead of his official election for Speaker on the House floor, working to eliminate opposition and secure the strongest leadership mandate possible in a razor-thin majority. In the latest development, Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Incoming GOP senator reveals how he will 'strap rocket-boosters' to Trump's agenda in new Congress
item.description: GOP Sen.-elect Tim Sheehy spoke to Fox News Digital about the issues he plans to prioritize when he is sworn in as a member of the Senate next month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mayor Adams on City of Yes plan passing City Council vote
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council has approved Mayor Eric Adams' ambitious City of Yes plan. The $5 billion plan will rezone the Big Apple for the first time in six decades. It's estimated to create more than 80,000 new housing units over 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump’s position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC that focused on advertising likening Donald Trump’s stance on abortion to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump names former Sen. David Perdue of Georgia to be ambassador to China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has selected former Sen. David Perdue of Georgia to be the U.S. ambassador to China, leaning on a former business executive turned politician to serve as the administration's envoy to America's most potent economic and military adversary.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How measles, whooping cough and worse could roar back on RFK Jr.’s watch
item.description: With anti-vaccine activist Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in charge of the nation’s biggest health agency, it wouldn’t take much tampering to enable vaccine-preventable diseases to make a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Arthur Allen, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates Rodney Scott to lead Customs and Border Protection
item.description: Rodney Scott at CBP and Caleb Vitello at ICE would work alongside Stephen Miller, who was named deputy chief of staff for policy and Tom Homan, also tapped to be a "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: France's Macron vows to stay in office till end of term, says he'll name a new prime minister soon
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron vowed Thursday to stay in office until the end of his term, due in 2027, and announced that he will name a new prime minister within days in efforts to overcome the political deadlock following the resignation of ousted Prime Minister Michel Barnier.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Anthony Salisbury as deputy Homeland Security advisor
item.description: Anthony Salisbury is currently a special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) in Miami.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump nominates Brandon Judd as ambassador to Chile
item.description: Brandon Judd was the president of the National Border Patrol Council, which represents around 18,000 Border Patrol agents and support personnel. He officially retired from the post on May 18.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Five new details in search for UnitedHealthcare CEO's shooter
item.description: New details emerged on Thursday as the manhunt entered its second day for the suspected gunman in the fatal shooting of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names David Sacks as AI, crypto czar
item.description: President-elect Trump said Thursday that venture capitalist David Sacks will serve as the White House artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptocurrency czar.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth faces senators' concerns not only about his behavior but also his views on women in combat
item.description: Minnesotan Pete Hegseth has spent the week on Capitol Hill trying to reassure Republican senators that he is fit to lead President-elect Donald Trump’s Department of Defense in the wake of high-profile allegations about excessive drinking and sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Missouri abortion ban slated to lift Friday if judge allows
item.description: A judge must decide whether “targeted regulation of abortion providers” laws are also unconstitutional under Amendment 3.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic rep pushes Biden to issue blanket pardon to those Patel could target
item.description: He noted that law enforcement officers, military personnel and others are at risk of extreme legal battles.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son sparks calls for granting more clemencies
item.description: President Biden’s surprise pardon of his son Hunter has prompted calls for the outgoing commander in chief to grant clemency to other Americans before he hands over the White House to President-elect Trump next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: International students confused, anxious about their futures under second Trump term
item.description: International students are holding their breath as President-elect Trump gears up to return to the White House. Multiple schools have put out guidance on when foreign-born students should and shouldn't go abroad to ensure they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House panel votes to absorb legal fees of DFLer in employment dispute
item.description: A DFL state lawmaker will have legal fees covered by the Minnesota House after alleging her rights were violated by an outside employer. Republicans say the move sets a bad precedent.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: St. Cloud appoints first Somali American to city council
item.description: On a unanimous vote, the city council appointed Hudda Ibrahim to fill a vacancy on the seven-member board. She replaces Jake Anderson, who was elected St. Cloud mayor last month.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: The platform’s features are based off of Twitter, and they include the ability to message fellow users as well as a discover and feed tab.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump falls just below 50% in popular vote, but gets more than in past elections
item.description: This year's popular-vote margin is the second-closest since 1968 and still tightening. It shows just how closely divided the country is politically, and that any shift to the right is marginal.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump taps investment banker Warren Stephens for UK ambassador
item.description: President-elect Trump announced on Monday that investment banker Warren Stephens would serve as U.S. ambassador to the United Kingdom.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Some Democrats are frustrated over Joe Biden reversing course and pardoning his son Hunter
item.description: Already reeling from their November defeat at the polls, Democrats now are grappling with President Joe Biden's pardoning of his son for a federal felony conviction — after the party spent years slamming Donald Trump as a threat to democracy who operates above the law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: President-elect Trump plans to nominate Kash Patel to serve as head of the FBI, elevating a staunch ally and controversial figure who sparked internal battles during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Trudeau flies to Florida to meet with Trump after tariffs threat
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau flew to Florida on Friday to have dinner with President-elect Donald Trump at his Mar-a-Lago club after Trump threatened to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC
